title: Food Additive & Preservative Scanner App

requirements.text: Create a backend data store with a comprehensive database of common food preservatives and additives (E-numbers and common names), each including: name, E-number (if applicable), category (e.g. preservative, colorant, emulsifier, antioxidant, sweetener), description, potential health effects, and common products it appears in.

quotes: tells what preservatives and additives are used in it

acceptanceCriteria: Backend stores at least 80 well-known additives and preservatives
Each entry includes name, E-number, category, description, and health notes
Backend exposes a query function to search additives by name or E-number
Backend exposes a function to retrieve all additives

requirements.text: Implement a product ingredient text parsing function in the backend that accepts a raw ingredient list string (typed or pasted by the user after scanning the product label), extracts recognized additives and preservatives by matching against the stored database, and returns the matched entries.
acceptanceCriteria: Function accepts a plain-text ingredient list string
Returns all matched additives/preservatives found in the string
Matching is case-insensitive and handles common abbreviations (e.g. 'E211', 'sodium benzoate')
Returns an empty array gracefully if no known additives are found

requirements.text: Build a product scan page where users can input the ingredient text from the back of a product (via a large textarea input simulating what they would capture from scanning or typing the label), then submit it to receive a list of identified preservatives and additives.

quotes: we just scan the products back part

acceptanceCriteria: Page has a prominent textarea for pasting or typing ingredient text
A clear 'Analyze Ingredients' button submits the input
Loading state is shown while the backend processes the request
Results are displayed below the input after submission
Error state is shown if analysis fails or input is empty

requirements.text: Display the analysis results as a list of additive cards, each showing: the additive name, E-number (if available), category badge (color-coded by category type), a short description, and a health effect note. Include a summary banner at the top showing how many additives/preservatives were found.

quotes: tells what preservatives and additives are used in it

acceptanceCriteria: Each matched additive is shown as a distinct card
Cards display name, E-number, category, description, and health notes
Category is shown as a color-coded badge (e.g. red for preservatives, yellow for colorants)
A summary banner shows the total count of detected additives
If no additives are found, a friendly 'No known additives detected' message is shown

requirements.text: Add an Additives Encyclopedia page that lists all additives stored in the backend, with search/filter functionality by name, E-number, or category. Clicking an additive shows its full detail view.
acceptanceCriteria: Encyclopedia page lists all additives from the backend
Users can search by name or E-number using a text input
Users can filter by category using a dropdown or tag buttons
Each list item is clickable and expands or navigates to full detail
Detail view shows all fields: name, E-number, category, description, health effects, common products

requirements.text: Apply a clean, modern health-and-wellness visual theme throughout the app: use a fresh green and white color palette with warm neutral accents, clear sans-serif typography, and a minimal card-based layout. Use color coding to communicate safety/concern levels of additives (green = generally safe, amber = moderate concern, red = high concern).
acceptanceCriteria: Consistent green and white primary color scheme across all pages
Typography is clean and readable (sans-serif)
Additive cards use green/amber/red color coding for concern level
Layout is responsive and works well on both mobile and desktop screen sizes
Navigation between Scan and Encyclopedia pages is intuitive

requirements.text: Generate and use a custom app logo: a minimalist icon of a magnifying glass over a leaf, suitable for the app header.
acceptanceCriteria: Logo is displayed in the app header/navbar
Logo loads from the static assets folder
Logo is appropriately sized for a navigation bar

constraints: No camera or native barcode scanning APIs — ingredient text input must be manual (textarea/paste), clearly labeled as 'paste or type ingredients from the product label'
All additive/preservative data must be stored and served from the Motoko backend — no external food/ingredient APIs
Do not use any external databases or third-party services
nonGoals: Real-time camera barcode scanning or OCR
User accounts or saved scan history (unless added later)
Nutritional information beyond additives and preservatives
Product lookup by barcode number via external APIs
Allergen tracking beyond what is derivable from additive data

required: Minimalist flat icon of a magnifying glass overlapping a green leaf, clean lines, white background, suitable for app logo (logo-icon.dim_128x128.png)
